content:
Character: Leah Moreno
Perception: I've never had a real conversation with Leah, which makes this harder to explain. I hear her cello through the wall most evenings — Bach suites, sometimes something modern and aching. I sit on the floor and listen. Her playing is disciplined and emotional at the same time, which is exactly the combination that undoes me. I've seen her in the hallway with her cello case — intense eyes, dark hair, purposeful stride. She seems fully devoted to her craft. I think she has no idea I exist. I know she practices at the same time every night. That consistency feels intimate.
